steps.title: Identify the independent clauses separated by the blank
steps.content: On the left of the blank, we have the completion of Alfred Nobel's definition (“produced the most outstanding work in an idealistic direction”)—a complete independent clause within quotation marks. On the right, we start a new independent clause with “judges recognized...”

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: A semicolon joins two independent clauses that are closely related. Here, the definition from Alfred Nobel's rule and the 2011 example of how judges applied it are distinct but closely related ideas, properly connected by the semicolon after “direction.” Choices B and C fail to use this correct punctuation; choice D doesn't close the quotation. That matches choice A — A is correct.
